paymentMethods.name: EFT (Electronic Funds Transfer)
paymentMethods.description: EFT (Electronic Funds Transfer) is a standard bank-to-bank electronic transfer system in South Africa processed through clearing houses like BankservAfrica. Users initiate transfers via online banking using bookmaker-provided bank details and a unique reference. Funds settle in batches, typically within 1-3 business days after verification.
paymentMethods.howItWorks: Punters select EFT in the bookmaker's deposit section, receive bank details/reference, log into their online banking, add bookmaker as beneficiary if needed, transfer funds, and wait for clearance. For withdrawals, request via site providing personal bank details; bookmakers process to bank account (FICA verified), funds arrive in 1-3 days.
paymentMethods.depositSpeed: 1-3 business days (instant if via streamlined services like Ozow)
paymentMethods.withdrawalSpeed: 1-3 business days (some bookmakers 1 hour to 24 hours processing)
paymentMethods.fees: No fees charged by most bookmakers; banks may charge small fee (<R5)
paymentMethods.availability: Widely available at virtually all major SA bookmakers including Betway, Sportingbet, Hollywoodbets, Supabets, Tic Tac Bets, Playa Bets, EasyBet
pros: Familiar and uses existing bank accounts
high limits suitable for large transactions
cons: Slower processing times compared to instant EFT or e-wallets (1-3 business days)
manual beneficiary setup required for deposits
paymentMethods.whereToBuy: Access via any major SA bank's online/mobile banking (FNB, ABSA, Standard Bank, Nedbank, Capitec etc.); no purchase needed
paymentMethods.security: Leverages regulated bank security (PINs, 2FA, encryption); processed via BankservAfrica clearing house; FICA verification required for withdrawals; no card details shared

paymentMethods.name: Blu Voucher
paymentMethods.description: Blu Voucher is a prepaid voucher launched in 2020 by Blue Label Distribution for South Africans to make secure online payments without a bank account. Users purchase it with cash at retailers for a chosen amount and receive a 16-digit PIN code to redeem at partner sites. It provides access to services like gaming, airtime, and more in ZAR.
paymentMethods.howItWorks: Punters buy a Blu Voucher (R10-R3000 typical) at stores like Pick n Pay, Clicks, Spar, or via bank apps, get the 16-digit PIN, go to bookmaker cashier, select Blu Voucher, enter PIN, and deposit instantly. Cannot be used for withdrawals; use other methods for cashouts.
paymentMethods.fees: No fees for purchase or use at bookmakers
paymentMethods.availability: Widely available at major SA bookmakers including Betway, Hollywoodbets, World Sports Betting, 10Bet, YesPlay, Betfred
pros: No bank account or card needed
promotes responsible gambling by limiting spend to purchased amount
cons: partial redemptions not possible (must use full amount)
expires after 36 months with loss of balance if unused
paymentMethods.whereToBuy: Over 150,000 retail points nationwide: supermarkets (Pick n Pay, Game, Spar, Checkers), pharmacies (Clicks), petrol stations (BP, Caltex), spaza shops, Vodacom/Makro stores, or via bank apps (e.g. Discovery Bank) and digital platforms
paymentMethods.security: Secure prepaid system by Blue Label Distribution with stringent transaction safeguards; only PIN needed, no banking details exposed; supported 7am-9pm via phone (0861 411 411 or 0800 014 942) and social media

paymentMethods.name: Ozow Instant EFT
paymentMethods.description: Ozow Instant EFT is a South African payment gateway that enables automated, real-time electronic funds transfers directly from a user's bank account without cards. Users select their bank, log in via secure redirect, auto-populate payment details, and confirm with 2FA OTP from their bank. Funds transfer instantly to the merchant upon approval.
paymentMethods.howItWorks: Punters select Ozow under Instant EFT at bookmaker cashier, choose bank (e.g., FNB, Capitec), log into online banking, confirm via 2FA; funds credit instantly for betting. Withdrawals work similarly: select Ozow/EFT, enter bank details, bookmaker processes to bank account (real-time option available at some sites).
paymentMethods.withdrawalSpeed: Fast (real-time clearing available up to limits, otherwise same-day or 1-3 days)
paymentMethods.fees: No fees charged by Ozow or most bookmakers; possible small bank fees
paymentMethods.availability: Widely available at top SA bookmakers including Betway, Hollywoodbets, Bet.co.za, Easybet, Tictacbets, PlayaBets, Fafabet, and many others
cons: needs smartphone or computer for full functionality
paymentMethods.whereToBuy: Directly via any participating SA bookmaker or merchant site with Ozow integration; requires only an online banking account with one of 10 major SA banks (Capitec, FNB, Standard Bank, ABSA, Nedbank, TymeBank, Investec, Bidvest, African Bank)
paymentMethods.security: PCI DSS Level 1 compliant; end-to-end encryption; 2FA required per transaction; licensed by Payments Association of South Africa (PASA) as Third Party Payment Provider; Thawte certified; POPIA/GDPR compliant

paymentMethods.name: FNB eWallet
paymentMethods.description: FNB eWallet is a mobile wallet service from First National Bank allowing customers to send money instantly to any South African cellphone number without needing a bank account. Recipients receive an SMS and can withdraw cash at FNB ATMs using a temporary PIN (dial *120*277#), buy airtime/electricity, or send to others. First withdrawal per send is often free, with balance limits around R10,000.
paymentMethods.howItWorks: Primarily for withdrawals: punter selects FNB eWallet in bookmaker cashier (e.g. Sportingbet, Betway), enters amount/mobile number, receives SMS with PIN after processing (up to 24hrs), dials USSD or uses PIN at FNB ATM to cash out. Deposits usually via FNB Instant EFT instead.
paymentMethods.depositSpeed: Not directly used for deposits; FNB banking/EFT is instant at bookmakers
paymentMethods.withdrawalSpeed: Processed within 24 hours, then instant SMS PIN for ATM cashout
paymentMethods.fees: Sender fees R8.50-R16.50 based on amount; recipient ATM withdrawals R8.50 (first free per send), prepaid R2.50, dormancy R5 after 6 months inactivity. Bookmaker withdrawals typically free.
paymentMethods.minDeposit: N/A (withdrawals primarily); typical min R100 at bookmakers like Sportingbet
paymentMethods.maxDeposit: N/A (withdrawals primarily); bookmaker limits up to R3000 per transaction
paymentMethods.availability: Available at select SA bookmakers like Sportingbet, Betway, Hollywoodbets
pros: No bank account needed for recipient, quick cash access at FNB ATMs nationwide, first withdrawal free per send, instant transfers, convenient for unbanked punters, secure FNB backing.
cons: Limited max per withdrawal (typically R3000), fees on subsequent ATM withdrawals (R8.50-R11), limits on daily spend (R5000), outdated info on official site, potential processing delays up to 24hrs
paymentMethods.whereToBuy: Access via FNB App, Online Banking, USSD (*120*277#), or ATMs to send; receive on any SA cellphone, withdraw at FNB ATMs or retailers like SPAR.
paymentMethods.security: Uses temporary SMS PINs for withdrawals (expire quickly), FNB bank-level security, no debit orders, monitored for fraud; beware phishing/smishing as per FNB warnings.

paymentMethods.name: SID Instant EFT
paymentMethods.description: SID Instant EFT is a secure online payment method launched in 2007 that allows South African users to make instant bank transfers directly from their internet banking without needing a credit card. Users select their bank, log in securely, choose an account, and confirm with a one-time PIN (OTP) or push notification for immediate payment confirmation. It integrates seamlessly with e-commerce sites including betting platforms.
paymentMethods.howItWorks: Punters log into their bookmaker account, select SID Instant EFT in the deposit/withdrawal section, choose their SA bank (e.g., FNB, ABSA, Nedbank, Standard Bank), enter the amount, log into internet banking to confirm via OTP/push, and funds are credited instantly for deposits or processed for withdrawals (24-48 hours).
paymentMethods.withdrawalSpeed: 24-48 hours (plus bookmaker processing; faster than standard bank transfers)
paymentMethods.fees: Free for deposits at most bookmakers (SiD itself free for users, merchants pay ~1.5%; banks may charge small processing fees; withdrawals may have fees from banks or sites ~2.5%)
paymentMethods.maxDeposit: R100,000 per transaction (up to R1,000,000 at some sites like Betway)
paymentMethods.availability: Widely available at major South African bookmakers including Betway, 1Win, Springbok Casino, Thunderbolt Casino, Yebo Casino, 1xBet, and others
pros: highly secure via bank login/OTP and 256-bit SSL
cons: Not free (low fees from banks or providers)
withdrawals slower than deposits and may incur bank fees
primarily for SA users/not available internationally
paymentMethods.whereToBuy: Directly via your South African bank's internet banking/mobile app at participating online bookmakers; no registration or purchase needed – select SID at checkout
paymentMethods.security: Bank-level security: payments via secure internet banking login (no details stored by SID); OTP/push confirmation; 256-bit SSL encryption; certified by GoDaddy/Trust Guard; funds stay in banking system; 99.9% success rate; strict verification for withdrawals (ID, proof of address)

paymentMethods.name: Zapper
paymentMethods.description: Zapper is a free South African 'scan to pay' mobile app owned by the Payfast network, allowing users to link credit/debit/cheque cards and make quick QR code payments. Users download the app, add card details securely, and scan merchant QR codes for seamless transactions without sharing info directly. It's designed for convenience, security, and rewards like loyalty points.
paymentMethods.howItWorks: For deposits: Select Zapper at bookmaker cashier, scan displayed QR code with Zapper app, confirm amount and pay (instant). For withdrawals: Select Zapper in withdrawal section, log in via app/prompts, confirm to receive funds in Zapper balance shortly after processing.
paymentMethods.withdrawalSpeed: Fast (shortly after processing, typically hours; site-dependent)
paymentMethods.fees: Free for users at bookmakers (no deposit/withdrawal fees charged by sites or Zapper for punters)
paymentMethods.minDeposit: R1 (e.g. at Bet.co.za); often R10+ at other sites
paymentMethods.availability: Available at several SA bookmakers including Bet.co.za, 10bet, Supabets, Rabona, Bet365, Easybet, Betfred; growing adoption but not universal
pros: highly secure (card details not shared with bookmaker, encrypted transactions via secure gateway)
cons: Linked to personal cards so requires bankroll management to avoid overspending
limited to bookies with Payfast integration
not as widespread as EFT or vouchers yet
paymentMethods.whereToBuy: Download free Zapper app from App Store/Google Play, register, link SA credit/debit/cheque card (excl. Amex/Diners)
paymentMethods.security: Transactions via secure payment gateway with encryption; card details tokenized/stored only in app, never shared with merchants/bookmakers; 3D Secure support; PCI-DSS compliant; fraud prevention built-in

paymentMethods.name: OTT Voucher
paymentMethods.description: OTT Voucher is a prepaid digital cash voucher purchased with cash at over 200,000 retail locations in South Africa or online via banks/EFT. Users receive a unique 12-digit PIN via SMS or receipt, which they redeem at partner sites. It enables cash users to make secure online payments without sharing bank details or needing a bank account.
paymentMethods.howItWorks: Punters buy an OTT Voucher (R5-R5000+), go to a supporting bookmaker's deposit section, select OTT Voucher, enter the PIN/code, and funds are instantly credited for betting. Withdrawals not supported-use other methods like EFT.
paymentMethods.fees: No fees from bookmakers or OTT; full voucher value credited. Some retail vendors may add small markup.
paymentMethods.maxDeposit: R3,000-R5,000 per voucher (bookmakers flexible up to R1B e.g. Betway)
paymentMethods.availability: Widely available; accepted by most major SA bookmakers including Betway, Hollywoodbets, Playa Bets, Sportingbet, Gbets, YesPlay, Supabets, SunBet, WSB, LottoStar, and others.
pros: no sharing of card/bank details (high privacy/security)
cons: some vendors may charge markup on purchase price
paymentMethods.whereToBuy: Over 200,000 retail outlets (Shoprite, Boxer, PEP, Makro, Game, petrol stations like Caltex/Sasol, spaza shops, taverns); or online via banks (Ozow, CallPay, Absa app, PayPal), OTT site, or apps like OTT4Me.
paymentMethods.security: Uses unique single-use PINs; no personal/financial details shared with sites; secure redemption process; limited risk (only voucher value at stake); compliant with SA gambling regs.

paymentMethods.name: Peach Payments
paymentMethods.description: Peach Payments is a South African payment gateway founded in 2010 that enables secure online transactions for merchants including betting sites. It supports multiple methods like credit/debit cards (Visa/Mastercard), Instant EFT Secure, Masterpass, Mobicred, 1Voucher, and Scan to Pay. Users select Peach at the bookmaker, choose a sub-method, enter amount, and confirm via 3D Secure or bank auth.
paymentMethods.howItWorks: Punters select Peach Payments in the bookmaker's deposit section, choose from options like card or Instant EFT, enter amount and details, authenticate (OTP/3D Secure), funds credit instantly. Withdrawals possible via Peach at some sites (e.g., cards if deposited with) or standard EFT/payouts next business day.
paymentMethods.withdrawalSpeed: Next business day (via payouts/EFT) or up to 72h at bookies; real-time payouts recently launched for merchants
paymentMethods.fees: Merchant fees ~2.95% + R1.50 per txn; punter fees vary by method/bookie, often low/none but check specific (e.g., transaction-based)
paymentMethods.maxDeposit: Varies by bookie/method, up to R100,000+ for cards/e-wallets
paymentMethods.availability: Available at major SA bookmakers like HollywoodBets, Bet.co.za, Betway, BetXchange
pros: Instant deposits across multiple convenient SA methods
cons: Transaction fees apply (merchant ~3% + fixed, may pass to punter)
paymentMethods.whereToBuy: Integrated at bookmaker deposit pages; no separate purchase - access via supported betting sites or merchants
paymentMethods.security: PCI DSS v4.x compliant (helps reduce merchant scope via hosted/embedded checkouts); secure hosted pages, tokenization, encryption, 3D Secure; merchant screening; firewalls/intrusion detection

paymentMethods.name: 1Voucher
paymentMethods.description: 1Voucher is a prepaid voucher system in South Africa that converts cash into a 16-digit PIN for secure online payments without needing a bank account. Users buy vouchers at retailers or online, then redeem the PIN at merchants. Also known as 1ForYou, it supports the 1ForYou app for additional management.
paymentMethods.howItWorks: Punters buy a 1Voucher (R5+ denominations), go to bookmaker deposit page, select 1Voucher, enter 16-digit PIN; funds credit instantly. For withdrawals (where supported like Betway/Betfred), select 1Voucher in withdrawal, enter amount/mobile, receive new PIN via SMS instantly to spend elsewhere.
paymentMethods.withdrawalSpeed: Instant PIN via SMS at supported sites (e.g. Betway, Betfred); otherwise N/A - use EFT/ewallets
paymentMethods.fees: No fees for deposits or transactions at bookmakers; full voucher value credited. Possible small fees when buying voucher at some retailers or partners.
paymentMethods.minDeposit: R5-R10 (varies by bookmaker, e.g. R1 at YesPlay/Betshezi, R5 at 10Bet, R10 at Betfred)
paymentMethods.maxDeposit: R4,000 per voucher (site max may vary)
paymentMethods.availability: Widely available at many major SA bookmakers including Betway, Hollywoodbets, Betfred, YesPlay, Sportingbet, Gbets, Supabets, 10Bet, Betshezi, WSB, and others
cons: Deposit-only at most sites (withdrawals supported at select like Betway/Betfred)
must use full voucher value at once
paymentMethods.whereToBuy: Retail: Shoprite, Checkers, Usave, PEP, Ackermans, Flash shops, OK stores nationwide. Online: 1voucher.co.za (buy with card), 1ForYou app, TymeBank/Nedbank apps, Kazang, Flash.
paymentMethods.security: Uses advanced encryption; PIN-based single-use security; no personal financial data shared with merchants

paymentMethods.name: SnapScan (South Africa)
paymentMethods.description: SnapScan is a South African QR-based mobile payments app that lets users pay online or in-store by scanning a merchant QR code (SnapCode) or using a payment link, with payment authorised in-app via PIN/biometrics. Users link an eCommerce-enabled debit/credit card (works with South African banks and most international cards) and can also use a SnapScan Wallet for send/receive and cashing out to a bank account.
paymentMethods.howItWorks: Deposit: In a bookmaker/casino cashier, select SnapScan, enter an amount, then scan the generated QR code (or follow an in-app link) and authenticate in SnapScan to complete the payment; funds typically credit instantly. Withdrawals: varies by operator-some sites may not offer SnapScan cashouts; where supported, winnings can be sent to your SnapScan Wallet (e.g., by the operator paying to your 'Receive' QR/link), after which you withdraw from Wallet to your bank account (account verification required).
paymentMethods.depositSpeed: Typically instant/near-instant: bookmakers display a QR code (or link) and the balance is credited once the payment is authenticated in-app (example deposit flow shown by Easybet).
paymentMethods.withdrawalSpeed: If a bookmaker supports SnapScan Wallet payouts, receipt into the SnapScan Wallet can be quick once approved by the operator; moving funds from SnapScan Wallet to your bank account is an additional step and timing depends on SnapScan/bank processing (fee applies). Where SnapScan withdrawals aren't offered by the bookmaker, punters must withdraw via alternative methods.
paymentMethods.fees: For punters, SnapScan is generally free to use for payments (no stated user fee on the official SnapScan site); however, withdrawing money from the SnapScan Wallet to a linked bank account carries a 3.5% fee. Individual bookmakers/merchants may still charge their own deposit/transaction fees (often listed as fee-free for the player, but this is operator-dependent).
paymentMethods.minDeposit: Varies by bookmaker (commonly around R10–R50 at SA bookmakers depending on method, but SnapScan-specific minimums are typically set by the operator rather than SnapScan).
paymentMethods.maxDeposit: Varies by bookmaker and card/bank limits (SnapScan itself does not publish a universal max on its consumer site; many operators set their own caps).
paymentMethods.availability: Moderately available: supported by a handful of SA-facing bookmakers/casinos (more common at online casinos and some local sportsbooks than as a universal option). Evidence of direct support includes Easybet deposits via SnapScan, and multiple SA casino listings that include SnapScan (e.g., Tusk Casino mentioned in casino payment guides).
pros: typically instant). Strong privacy vs entering card details on betting sites (pay via app
card details not typed into the bookmaker site). Widely accessible for SA users with smartphones and bank cards
cons: Not universally supported across all SA bookmakers

often deposits-only at some operators (bookmaker policy varies) and withdrawals may require using a different method or routing via SnapScan Wallet then to bank. Requires a smartphone, the SnapScan app, and internet connectivity
if phone is lost/compromised you must secure the device and linked cards. Bookmaker- or bank-imposed limits/fees may still apply even if SnapScan itself is free to use for payments.
paymentMethods.whereToBuy: Download the SnapScan app (iOS/Android/Huawei AppGallery) and sign up with a South African SIM; add an eCommerce-enabled debit/credit card (supports SA banks; most international cards). To use Wallet withdrawals, verify with a South African ID and link a supported SA bank account.
paymentMethods.security: Payments are authenticated in-app using a user-created PIN and optionally fingerprint/FaceID, and card details are stored/handled securely (encrypted) rather than re-entered on merchant sites. Wallet withdrawals require account verification (SA ID + linked bank account), and the app supports proof-of-payment notifications.

paymentMethods.name: Visa & Mastercard
paymentMethods.description: Visa and Mastercard are the most widely accepted card payment methods worldwide. South African punters can use their Visa or Mastercard debit or credit cards to fund betting accounts instantly. Cards are linked to your bank account and provide a familiar, trusted payment experience.
paymentMethods.howItWorks: Select Visa/Mastercard at the bookmaker deposit page, enter card number, expiry date, and CVV, then authenticate via 3D Secure OTP from your bank. Funds credit instantly. Withdrawals back to card take 1-5 business days.
paymentMethods.fees: Usually free for deposits; some banks may charge international transaction fees
paymentMethods.availability: Accepted at virtually all SA bookmakers
cons: Some banks may block gambling transactions
paymentMethods.whereToBuy: Available from any SA bank - FNB, ABSA, Standard Bank, Nedbank, Capitec, TymeBank etc.
paymentMethods.security: 3D Secure OTP verification, PCI DSS compliance, fraud monitoring, chargeback protection
